jokes.text: I thought it was about her boobs.
jokes.setup: TV promo describes Carmen Electra's show 'Boobs' as being about remedial reading students and their serious teacher
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation focuses on the obvious sexual meaning of the title
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - missing the high-minded premise for the obvious. Good character work but predictable setup.

jokes.text: Well, it's a cinch Springfield won't be that lucky city.
jokes.setup: News about NFL expansion to award new team to lucky city
jokes.punchline: Homer's pessimistic assessment of Springfield's chances - sets up his later crusade
jokes.explanation: Setup for the episode's main plot. Functional but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Yeah, the only thing we're known for is leading the country in heart attacks.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Springfield's reputation
jokes.punchline: Barney's matter-of-fact delivery of Springfield's morbid claim to fame
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Springfield's unhealthy population. Solid but unremarkable.

jokes.text: I've had enough of your stinkin' thinkin'. I, Homer Simpson, will personally bring the new pro football franchise to Springfield.
jokes.setup: Barney's pessimistic comment about Springfield
jokes.punchline: Homer's grandiose declaration and alliterative phrase 'stinkin' thinkin'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - the alliteration and sudden heroic resolve is very character-specific. Sets up the main plot well.

jokes.text: God, we have fun.
jokes.setup: Homer's dramatic declaration
jokes.punchline: Marge's dry, understated response to Homer's grandiosity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - the deadpan delivery and economy of words makes this very effective. Great reaction beat.

jokes.text: what the Hellmann's real mayonnaise?!
jokes.setup: Marge about to say 'what the hell' when she sees Homer's work
jokes.punchline: Interrupts her own curse with a mayonnaise brand name
jokes.explanation: Creative way to have Marge almost curse. The brand substitution is clever and very Simpsons-style absurd.

jokes.text: It's the duff beer krustyburger buzz cola costington's department store kwik-e-mart stupid flanders park.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining corporate naming rights for the stadium
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous and long corporate name ending with 'stupid flanders'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and the 'stupid flanders' insertion shows Homer's personal bias bleeding into his business plan.

jokes.text: Maybe Homer brush teeth first?
jokes.setup: Homer asking 'Homer do great?' after his presentation
jokes.punchline: Marge's tactful suggestion about his hygiene
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Homer's disheveled state after his week-long project binge.

jokes.text: I love L.A.
jokes.setup: Commissioner about to watch L.A.'s promotional video
jokes.punchline: His immediate reaction before even watching - suggesting he's already biased
jokes.explanation: Shows the fix is in from the start. Good setup for the unfair competition.

jokes.text: 'I'll have what she's having' was one of the lines.
jokes.setup: Rob Reiner introducing himself as director of When Harry Met Sally
jokes.punchline: His awkward way of referencing his own famous movie line
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of celebrity self-promotion. The awkward phrasing makes it slightly funny.

jokes.text: Springfield has Apu Nahasapeemapetilon.
jokes.setup: L.A.'s video highlighting their cultural attractions
jokes.punchline: Homer's counter-argument is just one person's difficult name
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between L.A.'s sophisticated pitch and Homer's simple, character-focused response.

jokes.text: Springfield takes in garbage from other cities.
jokes.setup: L.A. video: 'Hollywood takes in talented actors and writers from all over the world'
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest but unflattering parallel about Springfield
jokes.explanation: Excellent structural parallel that undermines Springfield's case. Homer's honesty works against him perfectly.

jokes.text: Some of hollywood's biggest stars have taken the time to sign waivers allowing celebrity impersonators to sing in this video.
jokes.setup: Rob Reiner promising celebrity endorsements
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're just impersonators with legal waivers
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of Hollywood's cheap tactics. The legal waiver detail makes it more specific and funny.

jokes.text: Springfield don't make nothin' because Springfield blows
jokes.setup: L.A. promotional song about their achievements
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest assessment of Springfield in song form
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. The musical format makes the insult more memorable.

jokes.text: I'm sick of L.A.! They got earthquakes, wildfires, bennifers, brangelinas, and that potty mouth, Sarah Silverman!
jokes.setup: Commissioner seems to favor L.A.
jokes.punchline: Sudden reversal listing L.A.'s problems, including celebrity couple portmanteaus
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from natural disasters to celebrity culture. The Sarah Silverman dig is unexpectedly specific.

jokes.text: I didn't kill my husband to get control of his franchise to give a team to Los Angeles!
jokes.setup: Commissioner explaining her decision against L.A.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of murder as business motivation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy. The casual delivery of a murder confession is perfectly absurd and unexpected.

jokes.text: Now if you'll excuse me, I gotta go tell my gay grandson I still love him.
jokes.setup: Commissioner wrapping up after her murder confession
jokes.punchline: Incongruously wholesome family obligation after dark revelation
jokes.explanation: Great tonal whiplash from murder confession to heartwarming family acceptance. The contrast is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I've got melt mania! I got downs syndrome!
jokes.setup: Fans showing enthusiasm for the Meltdowns team
jokes.punchline: Fan accidentally connects 'downs' from team name to medical condition
jokes.explanation: Problematic joke that relies on disability for humor. Poor taste even for The Simpsons' standards.

jokes.text: Who ya talkin' to? Your imaginary girlfriend? / Isn't your mother a well-known whore? / You win this round, Mel.
jokes.setup: Kent apparently talking to himself, gets mocked
jokes.punchline: Quick escalation to personal insults, then Kent's gracious defeat
jokes.explanation: Good rapid-fire exchange. Kent's gracious acceptance of defeat is the best part.

jokes.text: Did I ever tell you people about the time I met Lloyd Bridges? / Yeah, you passed him in the airport. / Yes, but which airport? / Hartsfield in Atlanta.
jokes.setup: Mayor starting to tell a story everyone's heard
jokes.punchline: Citizens know it so well they can recite the details, including the specific airport
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing how boring and repetitive Mayor Quimby is. The specific detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Criminals who prey on the elderly people who watch this program.
jokes.setup: 48 Minutes showing America's number one problem
jokes.punchline: Directly addressing their elderly audience as victims
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about the show's demographic and the scam about to unfold.

jokes.text: If i knock him out, I'll be a hero. And people will listen to my loose-brained nonsense.
jokes.setup: Abe realizes the commissioner matches the criminal description
jokes.punchline: Abe's honest self-assessment of his own mental state
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abe moment - self-aware about being ignored but still planning heroics. 'Loose-brained nonsense' is great phrasing.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, did I just say 'Wiggum'? I... I meant 'wigwam.'
jokes.setup: Wiggum reciting Longfellow poetry
jokes.punchline: Accidentally inserts his own name into the poem, then corrects himself
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Wiggum's ego bleeding into everything, even poetry recitation.

jokes.text: Maybe he's thinking of a new shape for the football.
jokes.setup: Discussion of why the commissioner didn't show up
jokes.punchline: Lisa's innocent but absurd suggestion for the delay
jokes.explanation: Nice absurd Lisa moment. The idea of redesigning football shape is charmingly naive.

jokes.text: Not so tough when someone hits you from behind with golf club, are ya?
jokes.setup: Grampa attacking who he thinks is a criminal
jokes.punchline: Grampa's tough-guy posturing after sucker-punching with golf club
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy with character consistency. Grampa's false bravado after cheap shot.

jokes.text: I thought he was trying to steal my jewels and all them pictures of Bert and Lucy.
jokes.setup: Abe explaining why he attacked the commissioner
jokes.punchline: His confused reference to 'Bert and Lucy' instead of grandchildren
jokes.explanation: Good Abe confusion. The wrong names for his grandchildren shows his mental state perfectly.

jokes.text: You're a useless old man. Name one thing you do for this family. / I watch the baby. / Where is the baby? / You left me with a baby?!
jokes.setup: Homer berating Abe for losing the football team
jokes.punchline: Abe's defense crumbles as he realizes he's lost the baby he was supposed to watch
jokes.explanation: Good escalation. Abe's one defense against being useless immediately backfires in the worst way.

jokes.text: Nobody loves you when you're old and gray / nobody needs you when you're upside down / everybody's hollering about their own birthday / everybody loves you when you're six foot in the ground.
jokes.setup: Abe feeling depressed and rejected
jokes.punchline: Morbid folk song about being appreciated only when dead
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective. The folk song format makes Abe's depression both sad and funny.

jokes.text: Euthanasia? No way! I want to die with dignity. Like slipping in the shower and then they find me two weeks later, swollen up so bad, they don't know if I'm man or sofa.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting euthanasia doctor to depressed Abe
jokes.punchline: Abe's rejection of assisted suicide in favor of grotesque natural death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy. The 'man or sofa' comparison is perfectly grotesque and memorable. Classic Abe morbidity.

jokes.text: Aw, I think you're cute, too.
jokes.setup: Doctor saying Abe is a good candidate for assisted suicide
jokes.punchline: Abe misinterprets the clinical assessment as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection. Abe's sweet misunderstanding of a death sentence is both touching and funny.

jokes.text: Yeah, and we're cleaning out the death machine today. A lot of gunk gets stuck in it.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining the 24-hour waiting period
jokes.punchline: Mundane maintenance concerns about the suicide machine
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor. Treating the death machine like any other appliance needing cleaning is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: If you go, can I have your blanket and your liver? / Blanket? Sure. / Liver? Never!
jokes.setup: Jasper asking for Abe's possessions if he dies
jokes.punchline: Abe's inconsistent generosity - willing to give blanket but protective of liver
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction. The arbitrary line Abe draws between acceptable and unacceptable requests is funny.

jokes.text: Well, first of all, they're all perfect. Also, when's the Cap'n crunch movie coming out? And will it be 'r' or 'hard r'?
jokes.setup: Homer on phone apparently being asked about movies
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical review philosophy and absurd cereal movie question with rating concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer absurdity. The Cap'n Crunch movie question with serious rating consideration is wonderfully random.

jokes.text: President visits Europe?! On my dime?!
jokes.setup: Abe looking for something to get mad about in the newspaper
jokes.punchline: His outrage at presidential travel expenses
jokes.explanation: Good Abe characterization. The old man outrage at government spending is very authentic to the character.

jokes.text: Who's Fred Kanickee? / My appointment before you. / Nice guy. / Just a little... / Little screwed up.
jokes.setup: Doctor listing famous suicide victims including unknown name
jokes.punchline: Casual description of recent suicide client as 'a little screwed up'
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor. The understatement about someone who just committed suicide being 'screwed up' is perfectly clinical.

jokes.text: I want to hear the Glenn Miller orchestra, and I want to see cops beating up hippies.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking what music and visuals Grampa wants while dying
jokes.punchline: Grampa's nostalgic combination of big band music and police violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. Perfect encapsulation of Grampa's nostalgia for 'good old days' including the dark parts.

jokes.text: I want to hear the Glenn Miller orchestra, and I want to see cops beating up hippies.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking what music and visuals Abe wants during his death
jokes.punchline: Abe's very specific, very Abe-like requests combining nostalgia with violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abe Simpson request. The combination of big band music and anti-hippie violence is exactly what he'd want.

jokes.text: I'll kill you all! / When the law's reversed. / Ha, I'd like to see you try! / When the law's reversed.
jokes.setup: Doctor threatening police who stopped the suicide
jokes.punchline: Both sides making threats conditional on legal changes
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist exchange. The conditional threats based on future legal changes is perfectly bureaucratic.

jokes.text: I think you know my brother-in-law, Fred Kanickee. / Hoo, boy.
jokes.setup: Reference to the previous suicide victim
jokes.punchline: Lou's weary recognition and the doctor's uncomfortable acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that adds dark dimension - Lou knows the suicide victim personally.

jokes.text: I'm dead! I never felt so alive! And I got my 16-year-old body back!
jokes.setup: Abe thinks he's dead and in the afterlife
jokes.punchline: Paradoxical feeling of being more alive when dead, plus age reversal fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast. The 'never felt so alive' when thinking he's dead is a nice paradox.

jokes.text: That's odd. You'd think they'd come back as the cows.
jokes.setup: Abe seeing hamburgers in what he thinks is the afterlife
jokes.punchline: His logic about reincarnation and karmic justice for food
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist logic about afterlife mechanics. Shows Abe trying to make sense of his situation.

jokes.text: Charlie Chaplin? They sure put you to work. / You said it. / And you can talk! / Good for you.
jokes.setup: Abe thinking he's meeting Charlie Chaplin in the afterlife
jokes.punchline: Treating the silent film star talking as a personal achievement
jokes.explanation: Nice reference to Chaplin's silent film career. Abe's encouragement is sweet and in character.

jokes.text: So who went berserk, fatso or the little guy?
jokes.setup: Abe seeing Laurel and Hardy impersonators
jokes.punchline: His casual assumption that comedy duos inevitably have violent breakdowns
jokes.explanation: Good cynical Abe observation. His assumption about comedy partnerships ending badly is very him.

jokes.text: God wants us to die of old age after years of pain and reduced mobility.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining why suicide is wrong
jokes.punchline: His cheerful description of God's plan being prolonged suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned - presenting horrible suffering as God's loving plan with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: You're all going to die in a pointless war.
jokes.setup: Abe singing uplifting Superman song to children
jokes.punchline: Interrupts positive message with grim war prediction
jokes.explanation: Excellent Abe moment. The tonal whiplash from inspirational to apocalyptic is perfectly him and expertly delivered.

jokes.text: No, wait, wait! Bullfighting is a cruel pseudo-sport. / Lisa's right. / It is a cool, super sport.
jokes.setup: Lisa objecting to bullfighting as cruel pseudo-sport
jokes.punchline: Mayor mishears and enthusiastically agrees it's a 'cool, super sport'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay and misdirection. Mayor Quimby's mishearing perfectly undermines Lisa's moral argument.

jokes.text: If the bull dies, the crowd goes wild. If grampa dies, the crowd goes wild. Either way, we make a fortune on souvenirs and snacks.
jokes.setup: Homer considering the bullfighting outcomes
jokes.punchline: His callous calculation that his father's death is equally profitable
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Homer logic. The equal enthusiasm for either death outcome is callously hilarious.

jokes.text: Can we bring outside food? / Not even gum.
jokes.setup: Fan asking about stadium food policy during discussion of death spectacle
jokes.punchline: Homer's strict concession policy even for potentially deadly event
jokes.explanation: Good priorities joke. The focus on concession revenue during death discussion is perfectly capitalistic.

jokes.text: Ole! A penny. This is my lucky day.
jokes.setup: Abe practicing bullfighting moves
jokes.punchline: Finding a penny during dangerous bull practice makes him feel lucky
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast. Abe's small-scale optimism while preparing for mortal danger is very him.

jokes.text: We'll bring you the bull's heart, sweetie.
jokes.setup: Lisa expressing concern about bullfighting
jokes.punchline: Homer's grotesque promise to bring her the bull's organ
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that misses the mark. Homer's attempt at romantic gesture with organs is gross more than funny.

jokes.text: Like the fear I feel that I have left the iron on at home. Have I? Have I?!
jokes.setup: Announcer describing Abe's fearlessness in the ring
jokes.punchline: Contrasts with announcer's own domestic anxiety spiraling into panic
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between types of fear. The announcer's mundane worry escalating during dangerous spectacle works well.

jokes.text: I'd look pretty stupid in this outfit walking around fighting oysters.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting Abe give up bullfighting
jokes.punchline: Abe's bizarre alternative of fighting different animals in matador costume
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Abe logic. The visual of matador vs. oysters is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: A girl named Linda. / No, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Announcer describing Abe's moral dilemma caused by a young girl
jokes.punchline: Gets the name wrong, then corrects himself
jokes.explanation: Simple mistake correction. Not particularly funny but functional for the story.

jokes.text: Hurry up and kill it! I have to be at the opera by 7:00!
jokes.setup: Audience watching bullfight
jokes.punchline: High culture commitment conflicting with bloodsport attendance
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between cultural sophistication and bloodthirsty impatience.

jokes.text: I don't get what he's doing, and I'm smart. Not book smart or street smart or brain smart, but something.
jokes.setup: Homer confused by Abe's bullfighting behavior
jokes.punchline: His attempt to claim intelligence while listing all the ways he's not smart
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-awareness. The escalating negation of different types of intelligence is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: I don't get what he's doing, and I'm smart. Not book smart or street smart or brain smart, but something.
jokes.setup: Homer confused by Grampa's bullfighting behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer's claim of intelligence while eliminating all types of intelligence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer moment. Perfect structure eliminating all categories of intelligence while claiming to have some undefined type.

jokes.text: Please, no. I have a wife and three girlfriends.
jokes.setup: Man pleading for his life as bull chases him
jokes.punchline: His confession reveals he's been cheating extensively
jokes.explanation: Good escalation. The revelation of multiple affairs during life-threatening moment adds dark comedy.

jokes.text: If you let me go, I give you free large pizza pie with purchase of same. Offer not good tuesday through sunday. We're closed on monday.
jokes.setup: Luigi pleading with bull for his life
jokes.punchline: His terrible promotional offer that's essentially useless due to restrictions
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist business logic. Luigi's terrible promotional terms even during mortal peril is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You're just here to test the microphone. / Fred Wilson, my brother Bill, Dom Dimaggio, the paper boy, Howdy Doody, Josef Stalin...
jokes.setup: Aide explaining Abe is just testing the microphone
jokes.punchline: Abe continues naming 'communists' including fictional characters and Stalin himself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and historical reference. Abe's Red Scare paranoia including Howdy Doody is perfectly absurd.
